text:
On Novig, a parlay is a series of linked legs—each leg is matched against another user or users on the exchange. You're betting against counterparties, not the house.

Here's how it works: you build your parlay and submit it. Each leg gets priced based on what other users are willing to offer (the order book). You don't need one person to take all legs—each leg can be matched against different counterparties. Once all legs hit, your winnings roll to the next leg automatically.

The price you see reflects real supply and demand from other users, not a sportsbook's margin. There's no commission on winning bets (maker-taker model).

The main difference from a sportsbook: you're negotiating odds with other bettors in real time, not accepting a fixed price from a house.

Novig Support
